description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Mississippi (  MISS-iss-IP-ee) is a state in the Southeastern and Deep South regions of the United States.

aliases: Supreme Court of the United States of America
description: highest court of jurisdiction in the United States of America
description_wiki: The Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) is the highest court in the federal judiciary of the United States.

entity: Fourteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution
description: 1868 amendment addressing citizenship rights, civil and political liberties
description_wiki: The Fourteenth Amendment (Amendment XIV) to the United States Constitution was adopted on July 9, 1868, as one of the Reconstruction Amendments.

description: state and local laws enforcing racial segregation in the Southern United States
description_wiki: The Jim Crow laws were state and local laws introduced in the Southern United States in the late 19th and early-to-mid-20th centuries that enforced racial segregation.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: city in and county seat of Wayne County, and largest city in State of Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Detroit (  dih-TROYT, locally also  DEE-troyt) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Michigan.

description: city in Wayne County, Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Hamtramck (  ham-TRAM-ik) is a city in Wayne County in the U.S. state of Michigan.
The University of Detroit Mercy is a private Catholic university in Detroit, Michigan, United States.

surface_forms: Meharry Medical College's School of Dentistry
description_wiki: Meharry Medical College is a private historically black medical school affiliated with the United Methodist Church and located in Nashville, Tennessee.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.
World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

aliases: The Entertainment Capital of the World
The Gambling Capital of the World
description: seat of Clark County, and largest city in state of Nevada, United States
description_wiki: Las Vegas, colloquially referred to as Vegas, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Nevada and the seat of Clark County.

entity: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
aliases: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description: civil rights organization in the United States

aliases: Reconstruction era of the United States
description_wiki: The Reconstruction era, often simply called Reconstruction, was a period in United States history that followed the American Civil War (1861–1865) and was dominated by the legal, social, and political challenges of the abolition of slavery and federal control over, and reintegration of, the former Confederate States into the United States.

description: policy of promoting members of groups that have previously suffered from discrimination
description_wiki: Affirmative action (also sometimes called reservations, alternative access, positive discrimination or positive action in various countries' laws and policies) refers to a set of policies and practices within a government or organization seeking to address systemic discrimination.
